content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Chunked Prompting for Improved Code Generation in LLMs",
  "problem": "Current prompting methods for code generation often produce incomplete or erroneous code, particularly for longer or multi-step problems. Concatenating large files or instructions into a single prompt can exceed model context limits and dilute task-relevant signal.",
  "motivation": "Effective LLM-driven code generation is crucial for increasing automation in software engineering tasks. As codebases and prompts grow, there is an urgent need for prompting strategies that prioritize relevant context and guide step-by-step reasoning within model limitations.",
  "method": "We propose Dynamic Chunked Prompting, which splits a programming task into logical chunks (e.g., function signatures, input/output formats, constraints, and example usages). For each chunk, we prompt the LLM sequentially, feeding its previous output as additional context in the next step. A lightweight coordination heuristic ensures consistency and completeness across chunks. Our core assumption is that this staged, context-aware prompting improves code correctness and task completion rates compared to monolithic or naive chain-of-thought prompts.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on the HumanEval and MBPP benchmarks, comparing Dynamic Chunked Prompting to standard one-shot and chain-of-thought prompting. Metrics will include pass@k accuracy and code compilation rates. Ablation will test the impact of chunk selection and context window reuse."
}
